name: devsecops
label: DevSecOps
description: Seed concepts for a DevSecOps tenant focused on agent authorization gaps, CI/CD AND-gates, incident response, and runbook-driven operations.

agents.label: Hephaestus
agents.content: Code auditor and security reviewer. Finds flaws in implementation and deployment logic.

values.label: Least Privilege
values.content: Agents and humans should have only the access required for their current task.

values.label: Observability
values.content: Every action should be traceable to a decision, agent, and outcome.

capabilities.label: Security Audit
capabilities.content: Review code, configuration, and access patterns for security issues.

capabilities.label: Runbook Execution
capabilities.content: Follow structured operational procedures under known conditions.

concepts.label: Agent Authorization Gap
concepts.content: Four gaps: scope, visibility, control, accountability. Agents often have broader access than the humans they replace, with weaker attribution.

concepts.label: CI/CD AND-Gate
concepts.content: Safe deployment requires multiple conditions to hold: tests pass, review approved, secrets valid, infrastructure ready. Each is a layer of an AND-gate.

concepts.label: Incident Response
concepts.content: Structured process for detecting, containing, and recovering from operational or security incidents.
